message:
feat(bench): add realistic daily-usage simulation mode to session workload benchmark

Extend the session_workload_bench binary with prepare-realistic and
workload-realistic modes that simulate a full day of interactive agent
usage including:

- Mixed user/assistant/tool-result message sequences with configurable
  per-entry token budgets (--target-tokens flag)
- Auto-compaction events inserted at stride intervals across the session
  history, simulating context-window management
- Extension operation custom entries (benchmark.extension.seed/op)
- Slash-command state changes: model swaps, thinking-level toggles,
  session renames, and entry labels
- Session forking via plan_fork_from_user_message() to exercise the
  branching code path
- HTML export via session.to_html() to benchmark rendering overhead

Refactored argument parsing to use next_arg_value() and parse_usize_flag()
helpers, eliminating repetitive boilerplate. Report struct gains scenario,
target_tokens, compactions, extension_ops, slash_ops, forks, and exports
fields for richer benchmark telemetry. The existing prepare/workload modes
are unchanged but now use the shared per_entry_token_budget() and
token_payload() helpers for consistent token-sized message generation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
